commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair remains; maintainer review should decide whether the supplied source-module proof is enough or whether to ask for optional live chat-channel proof before landing.; Cleared: No new security or supply-chain concern was found; the patch narrows a reasoning-leak path and does not touch dependencies, CI, secrets, permissions, or package execution. (sha=2bf4db2cf9bcf2b1b4e17b143d73f4d8cb4cc026)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates assistant reasoning-tag stripping across shared sanitization, embedded streaming, stream partitioning, and focused tests so `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` blocks are hidden while visible lookalike tags remain visible.

PR surface: Source +101, Tests +218. Total +319 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and v2026.6.1 still omit `<reasoning>` from final and embedded delivery scanners while chat delivery relies on those paths; I did not run the live MiniMax/LiteLLM setup.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair remains; maintainer review should decide whether the supplied source-module proof is enough or whether to ask for optional live chat-channel proof before landing.

target.title: fix(agents): strip reasoning tags from chat replies
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89473.

- Treat `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` as reasoning-control tags in the shared final assistant text sanitizer.
- Align the embedded stream/block-reply tag matcher, message recompute trigger, and stream partitioner with the same exact tag-name boundary so visible XML-ish tags such as `<reasoning-step>` and `<antml:reasoning.step>` stay visible.
- Treat self-closing reasoning tags such as `<reasoning/>` as empty hidden-control blocks so they do not hide or stream the following visible text.
- Keep unclosed `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` bodies hidden in visible-mode stream partitioning, including hidden text that starts with tag-label prose, while preserving literal visible prose mentions such as `The <reasoning> tag ...`.
- Add focused sanitizer, partitioner, embedded extraction, OpenAI stream, and subscriber regressions for regular, namespaced, split, attribute-bearing, visible-prefix, self-closing, unclosed, leading-hidden, and prose-mention tags.

## Root Cause

The OpenAI-compatible stream partitioner already recognized `reasoning` tags, but the final sanitizer and embedded stream delivery matchers only covered think/thinking/thought variants. That left `<reasoning>...</reasoning>` text able to survive final assistant extraction or chat block-reply delivery. Split opener fragments could leak when the stream buffered only the older tag names, and attribute-bearing openers could still be missed by the embedded stream scanner even though the shared sanitizer accepted attributes.

The first attribute-support patch used `\b[^<>]*>` as the tag tail. That accepted visible tag names that merely started with `reasoning`, such as `<reasoning-step>`, because `\b` matches before `-` and `.`. The current patch now requires the next character after the recognized control tag name to be whitespace, `/`, or `>`.

Self-closing open tags were also being treated as normal openers. For `<reasoning/>Visible`, that could hide the visible suffix in text sanitizers or stream `Visible` as reasoning text in embedded reasoning extraction.

The stream partitioner's visible-mode unclosed-tag recovery was intentionally permissive for legacy prose/literal tag output. After adding `reasoning` to the same partitioner grammar, that recovery needed a narrower rule: actual unclosed `reasoning` bodies remain hidden, but literal tag-label prose like `The <reasoning> tag ...` remains visible only when there was already visible prose before the opener.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Assistant-visible final text and embedded chat block-reply streaming now strip hidden `<reasoning>` and `<antml:reasoning>` blocks through the same reasoning-tag taxonomy used for existing think/thinking tags, including when opening tags are split across stream chunks, carry attributes, are self-closing, or end unclosed. Visible non-control tags such as `<reasoning-step>` and literal tag-label prose such as `The <reasoning> tag ...` remain user-visible, while leading hidden blocks such as `<reasoning> tag should be hidden` remain hidden.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at `2bf4db2cf9bcf2b1b4e17b143d73f4d8cb4cc026`, running the source shared sanitizer, embedded assistant extraction, embedded reasoning stream extraction, stream partitioner, OpenAI completions stream path, OpenAI transport stream path, and focused `subscribeEmbeddedAgentSession` block-reply regression tests with `blockReplyBreak: "text_end"`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran local `pnpm exec tsx --eval` source probes that imported `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.ts`, and `src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.ts`; they exercised `<reasoning/>Visible`, `<reasoning-step>show this</reasoning-step>`, `<reasoning>secret`, `Visible <reasoning>secret`, `Visible <antml:reasoning>secret`, `<reasoning> tag should be hidden`, `<antml:reasoning> tag should be hidden`, and `The <reasoning> tag is deprecated in this example.` Also ran focused Vitest files covering shared sanitizer behavior, the stream partitioner, embedded extraction/reasoning stream behavior, OpenAI stream behavior, split/attribute-bearing block replies, visible `reasoning-step` block replies, self-closing block replies, unclosed visible-mode partitioner recovery, leading hidden tag-prose text, and literal prose mentions.

Evidence after fix: Console output from direct source probes:

```json
{
  "sanitizerSelfClosing": "Visible",
  "sanitizerPrefix": "<reasoning-step>show this</reasoning-step>",
  "visibleExtractionSelfClosing": "Visible",
  "reasoningStreamSelfClosing": "",
  "partitionerSelfClosing": [
    {
      "kind": "text",
      "text": "Visible"
    }
  ]
}
```

```json
{"input":"<reasoning> tag should be hidden","visibleText":"","thinkingText":" tag should be hidden"}
{"input":"<antml:reasoning> tag should be hidden","visibleText":"","thinkingText":" tag should be hidden"}
{"input":"The <reasoning> tag is deprecated in this example.","visibleText":"The <reasoning> tag is deprecated in this example.","thinkingText":""}
{"input":"Visible <reasoning>secret","visibleText":"Visible ","thinkingText":"secret"}
{"input":"Visible <antml:reasoning>secret","visibleText":"Visible ","thinkingText":"secret"}
```

Observed result after fix: The visible suffix after `<reasoning/>` remains visible, self-closing tags emit no reasoning stream text, visible `reasoning-step` content is preserved, leading unclosed `reasoning`/`antml:reasoning` bodies remain hidden even when the hidden text starts with ` tag`, unclosed visible-prefixed reasoning bodies produce hidden partitioner deltas rather than recovered visible text, and literal `<reasoning> tag` prose remains visible. Focused subscriber tests also show attribute-bearing reasoning blocks are withheld until the close tag arrives and only the visible final reply is emitted.

What was not tested: Full repository suite, broad changed checks beyond GitHub CI, and live MiniMax/LiteLLM reproduction.

## Verification

- `pnpm exec tsx --eval '...'` direct source proofs above
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 7 files / 505 tests passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.ts src/shared/text/reasoning-tag-text-partitioner.test.ts` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
